root_text.an4.25:1.1: “Nayidaṁ, bhikkhave, brahmacariyaṁ vussati janakuhanatthaṁ, na janalapanatthaṁ, na lābhasakkārasilokānisaṁsatthaṁ, na itivādappamokkhānisaṁsatthaṁ, na ‘iti maṁ jano jānātū’ti.
root_text.an4.25:1.2: Atha kho idaṁ, bhikkhave, brahmacariyaṁ vussati saṁvaratthaṁ pahānatthaṁ virāgatthaṁ nirodhatthanti.

translation_text.an4.25:1.1: “Mendicants, this spiritual life is not lived for the sake of fawning or flattering people, nor for the benefit of possessions, honor, or popularity, nor for the benefit of winning debates, nor thinking, ‘So let people know about me!’
translation_text.an4.25:1.2: This spiritual life is lived for the sake of restraint, giving up, fading away, and cessation.
translation_text.an4.25:2.1: The Buddha taught the spiritual life
translation_text.an4.25:2.3: but for the sake of restraint and giving up,
translation_text.an4.25:2.4: and because it culminates in extinguishment.
translation_text.an4.25:2.5: This is the path followed by the big-hearted,
translation_text.an4.25:3.2: as it was taught by the Buddha,
